The plant is also known as the Seychelles rosewood or Seychelles satinwood. Macphersonia chapelieri is a medium to large evergreen tree that can grow up to 15 meters tall. The tree is valued for its timber, which is used for furniture-making and other wood products.

intro: Macphersonia chapelieri, also known as Chapelier's tree, is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Cunoniaceae. The health benefits of Macphersonia chapelieri include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
